mission.title: knative: minScale should not apply to non-routable revisions
mission.description: minScale should not apply to non-routable revisions. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current knative deployment
steps.description:
Verify your knative version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n knative -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative
helm list -n knative 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working knative installation.

steps.title: Review knative configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant knative configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n knative -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative
kubectl get configmap -n knative -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=knative
```
This is a repost of #2720, except the request is that we actually actually fix the behavior and not just document that it works in a broken (unexpected) way.

steps.title: Apply the fix for minScale should not apply to non-routable revisions
steps.description:
* Ignore `minScale` annotation on `PodAutoscalers` if `Reachability` is `Ureachable`

**Release Note**

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n knative -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative
kubectl get events -n knative --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "minScale should not apply to non-routable revisions" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
* Ignore `minScale` annotation on `PodAutoscalers` if `Reachability` is `Ureachable`

**Release Note**

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with knative installed or the issue environment reproducible.
